# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert

Prevent command injection.
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :validate_params, only: :convert

  def convert
    uploaded_file = params[:image]
    target_format = params[:format]
    target_size = params[:size]

    # Secure the input to prevent command injection
    safe_format = target_format.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z]/, '')
    safe_size = target_size.gsub(/[^0-9x]/, '')

    input_path = uploaded_file.path
    output_path = Rails.root.join('public', "converted_image.#{safe_format}")

    system("convert #{Shellwords.escape(input_path)} -resize #{Shellwords.escape(safe_size)} #{Shellwords.escape(output_path)}")

    send_file output_path, type: "image/#{safe_format}", disposition: 'inline'
  end

  private

  def validate_params
    unless params[:image].respond_to?(:path) && params[:format].is_a?(String) && params[:size].is_a?(String)
      render plain: 'Invalid parameters', status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end
end
